summary: Newly synthesized glucose-6-phosphate remains undegraded because it cannot cross membranes directly from the cytoplasm into areas containing active glucose-6-phosphatases; this prevents wasteful energy expenditure before its use or storage as needed within cells like hepatocytes. The specific location of these crucial enzymes outside the main site of sugar production maintains cellular efficiency through controlled metabolism processes. Agent2 reasoned that due to the spatial separation between glycolytic products formed in the cytoplasm and their corresponding catabolizing enzymes found mainly in other compartments such as the ER, fresh glucose-6-phosphate stays unconverted initially to avoid unnecessary depletion when more regulated conditions are desired downstream. This preserves optimal physiological function without premature utilization steps which could otherwise occur if all substrates were freely accessible everywhere inside every type of tissue at once.

Answer choice: B
